retry_reason: The refcount operation (kref_init) is unconditional and always succeeds; the only failure mode is memory allocation failure, which is not a refcount issue and is not retryable by repeating the same call.
contract_summary: This function creates a new fbnic_fw_completion object, initializing its reference count to 1 via kref_init, and setting up a completion for synchronization. The caller receives ownership of a single reference and must eventually call kref_put (or the appropriate destructor) to release it. If allocation fails, NULL is returned. The caller should check the return value for NULL and handle accordingly. After obtaining a valid pointer, the caller is responsible for eventually decrementing the reference count exactly once to avoid leaks.
